cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking what the author did when he met Mr. Lee. First, I'll go back to the passage to refresh my memory. The author mentions that when he was ten years old, he was walking down the street with his mother and they stopped to speak to Mr. Lee. The author says, "I knew I could see Mr. Lee any time around the neighborhood, so I didn't pay any attention to him." So here, the key detail is that the author didn't pay attention to Mr. Lee. The mother then later tells him not to walk by someone without speaking, using the example of a dog wagging its tail. The author's action here was not to greet or speak to Mr. Lee at that time because he didn't think about it. The question is about what he did when he met Mr. Lee, which was to ignore him. Looking at the options: (A) He talked with somebody else. The passage doesn't mention him talking to someone else; he was with his mother, but the focus is on Mr. Lee. (B) He was attracted by a dog. The dog is part of the mother's advice, but the author wasn't actually looking at a dog at that time. (C) He greeted him politely. No, he didn't
